channel.title: Thoreau Daily Journal
channel.description: A daily entry from Henry David Thoreau's journals, delivered fresh each day.

item.title: Thoreau Journal - July 02, 1840
item.description:
July 2. I am not taken up, like Moses, upon a mountain to learn the law, but lifted up in my seat here, in the warm sunshine and genial light.

They who are ready to go are already invited.

Neither men nor things have any true mode of invitation but to be inviting.

Can that be a task which all things abet, and to postpone which is to strive against nature?
